methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $232M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Redwood Materials
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $6B+

data.narrative:
Lunar Energy, founded by Kunal Girotra, is a Series D-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector that has raised $232M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five private companies at Series C and Series D stages—Redwood Materials ($6.0B), KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Watershed ($1.8B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M)—spanning the broader frontier-tech energy landscape alongside one public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods apply to this stage. The stage-progression approach, using total capital raised as a baseline proxy and applying a 1.5x multiplier typical for Series D-to-next-stage advancement, implies a $348M valuation. The peer-median method anchors to the $1.8B median valuation across the five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which range from $700M to $6.0B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within the climate-tech cohort.

These methods triangulate to a range of $296M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The stage-progression method produces the conservative bound; peer medians drive the upper range. Key uncertainties include the appropriate peer weighting given significant valuation dispersion in the comp set, the stage-specific expectations of Lunar Energy's investors, and the company's progress against technology and commercialization milestones relative to named peers.
